findings.evidence: Between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:153 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:185, the fuel-yard scene runs through Nels's entire negotiation almost entirely as external description and short dialogue beats before Silas's interiority returns at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:187.
findings.problem: The chapter briefly loses its close-third pressure in the fuel-yard exchange. After an interior-rich opening, this 10+ paragraph run reads more like a camera following supply logistics than a damaged focalizer absorbing what his word has done to the city.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the passage around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:151, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:171, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:179, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:183 so the negotiation keeps returning to Silas-specific interior experience: bodily response to the queue, shame at seeing his lettering on the wrappers, private arithmetic linking the purchase back to Junie's kitchen, and a self-indicting reaction before he buys the packets.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if a reader can point to at least three distinct Silas-only interior beats in the revised fuel-yard scene spanning snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:151 through snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:199, with one beat before Nels reveals the uphill carts, one after that reveal, and one before the purchase; FAIL if the exchange still runs mostly as exterior action and dialogue until the buy is complete.

findings.evidence: The narration repeatedly uses clipped fragments at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:275 ("Too much breathing room in the design. Too much confidence..."), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:367 ("Tins into the chest. Chalks wrapped in old proofs..."), and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:395 ("Gold in the upper windows. Gold at the packet stalls...").
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping. These verbless inventory shards and emphatic fragments create pressure fast, but in repeated use they read like authorial shorthand rather than fully realized prose and undercut the stated style constraints.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the fragment runs anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:275, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:367, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:395 into grammatically complete sentences with explicit subjects and verbs. Keep the accumulation effect, but make the compression come from sentence design rather than omitted grammar.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:275, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:367, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:395 read as full clauses on the page and no longer depend on verbless fragments such as "Too much...," "Tins into...," or "Gold in..." for rhythm; FAIL if any of those local beats still rely on clipped fragment syntax.

findings.evidence: The same "as if" comparison scaffold recurs throughout the chapter at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:7, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:57, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:209, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:217, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:229, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:285, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:297, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:351, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:397.
findings.problem: The individual images are often strong, but the chapter over-relies on the same sentence hinge. Once the reader hears this comparison engine that many times, different descriptive beats start arriving with identical machinery and the prose loses some of its elasticity.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the image delivery in the opening kitchen and shop-loss scenes. Recast several of the repeated "as if" beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:7, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:33, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:209, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:229, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:297, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:351 into direct sensory statements, appositive metaphor, or action-based imagery so the syntax stops repeating the same move.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised opening cluster anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:5 and the eviction cluster anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_14.md:281 no longer stack multiple "as if" comparisons in close succession, and a reader can see that at least three of the cited beats have been recast into different syntactic shapes; FAIL if either scene still depends on repeated back-to-back "as if" clauses to carry its descriptive force.

summary: Booker PASS: the chapter cleanly executes the planned aftermath collapse, moving from Junie's kitchen arithmetic to public household fallout to the loss of Silas's shop in a strong causal chain. Dialogue PASS: Junie, Silas, Nels, Brindle, Otis, and Bess remain distinct, contraction use stays in register, and no example-line reuse, tense drift, or continuity contradiction surfaced against the supplied canon. Craft and prose FAIL: the fuel-yard scene drops interior pressure for too long, the narration repeatedly slips into constitution-barred clipped fragments, and the prose leans on a repeated "as if" comparison scaffold often enough to become a tic. No sanitization, timidity, or intimacy-fade findings were triggered in this chapter.
